mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding household income and aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with PAYEMS monthly changes over a 3-year rolling window (Spearman rank correlation < 0.03), or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with rising PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% represents restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and debt-service costs, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the mean forward return is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated federal funds rates and rising unemployment jointly signal a Fed tightening into a weakening labor market, a combination that precedes recessionary earnings contractions and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and UNRATE does not produce a negative and significant coefficient (p < 0.10) in predicting 20-day forward returns, or the strategy Sharpe ratio over triggered periods is below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recoveries, accelerating housing starts (HOUST) signal a rebound in construction activity, household formation, and leveraged consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following a 10% rise in HOUST are not positive in at least 60% of occurrences during post-2008 recovery years, or the mean return is indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate revenue growth and margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Monotonic relationship between INDPRO monthly changes and 60-day forward returns breaks down (Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window), or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 1% signals a sudden contraction in consumer demand that reduces corporate revenue expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of triggered months, or the mean forward return after a >1% RSAFS drop is not statistically below zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) coinciding with falling retail sales (RSAFS) indicate a stagflationary squeeze on real household purchasing power and corporate margins, jointly forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and falling RSAFS fails to produce negative average 20-day returns over a 3-year rolling window, or the hit rate of negative returns under this joint signal falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In early-cycle recoveries, a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) reflects accelerating labor market absorption that outpaces wage pressures, expanding corporate profitability and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During early-cycle periods, S&P 500 60-day forward returns following a 0.2pp drop in UNRATE are not positive in at least 60% of instances, or the mean return is statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05).
